# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server";

export function middleware(req: NextRequest) {
  const token = req.cookies['token'];
  
  if (!token) {
    return new Response('Unauthorized', { status: 401 });
  } else {
    // Here you can add your own logic for validating the JWT, like using jsonwebtoken library to verify the token.
    try {
      // Replace this with actual implementation of token validation
      const isValid = validateToken(token);
      
      if (!isValid) {
        return new Response('Unauthorized', { status: 401 });
      }
    } catch (error) {
      console.log("Error while verifying the JWT token", error);
      return new Response('Unauthorized', { status: 401 });
    }
    
    // If everything is fine, continue to next middleware or route handler
    return NextResponse.next();
  }
}
